module.name: core/fec_encoder.py
module.purpose: Encodes n_data packets into (n_data + n_parity) packets using a systematic Cauchy matrix construction over GF(256).
module.theory: Systematic code: data packets are sent unchanged, parity packets are computed. Full encoding matrix = [Identity (n_data x n_data)] stacked on top of [Cauchy (n_parity x n_data)]. Cauchy matrix: C[j][i] = 1 / (x_j XOR y_i) in GF(256) where x_j = n_data + j, y_i = i (disjoint sets). Key property: every square sub-matrix of a Cauchy matrix is invertible, guaranteeing recovery from any n_data received packets. Parity computation per byte position: parity_j[pos] = SUM_i( C[j][i] * data_i[pos] ) in GF(256)

implementation.class: __init__(n_data_packets=4, n_parity_packets=4) — builds Cauchy matrix _build_cauchy_matrix() — C[j][i] = gf_inv((n_data + j) ^ i) encode_block(data_packets) — pads packets to same length, computes parity, returns [n_data original + n_parity parity] packets get_overhead() — returns (n_parity / n_data) * 100 get_encoding_matrix() — returns full (n_data+n_parity) x n_data matrix

module.name: core/fec_decoder.py
module.purpose: Recovers original n_data packets from any n_data received packets (out of n_data + n_parity total) using matrix inversion over GF(256).
module.theory: Recovery algorithm: 1. Identify which packets were received (need >= n_data) 2. Extract corresponding rows from encoding matrix → sub-matrix S 3. Invert S over GF(256) using Gauss-Jordan elimination → S_inv 4. For each byte position: multiply S_inv × received_bytes = original_data Fast path: if all n_data data packets present, skip matrix math entirely.

implementation.class: __init__(n_data_packets=4, n_parity_packets=4) _build_encoding_matrix() — reconstructs identity + Cauchy matrix decode_block(received_packets) — received_packets is list[bytes|None], returns (recovered_data, success) get_statistics() — returns blocks_decoded, blocks_failed, packets_recovered, success_rate

module.source_code:
from core.galois import gf_mul, gf_matrix_invert
class FECDecoder: def __init__(self, n_data_packets=4, n_parity_packets=4): self.n_data = n_data_packets self.n_parity = n_parity_packets self.total = n_data_packets + n_parity_packets self.encoding_matrix = self._build_encoding_matrix() self.blocks_decoded = 0 self.blocks_failed = 0 self.packets_recovered = 0
def _build_encoding_matrix(self): from core.galois import gf_inv matrix = [] for i in range(self.n_data): row = [0] * self.n_data row[i] = 1 matrix.append(row) for j in range(self.n_parity): row = [] for i in range(self.n_data): val = (self.n_data + j) ^ i row.append(gf_inv(val)) matrix.append(row) return matrix
def decode_block(self, received_packets): total = self.n_data + self.n_parity received_indices = [i for i in range(total) if received_packets[i] is not None] if len(received_indices) < self.n_data: self.blocks_failed += 1 return None, False data_present = all(received_packets[i] is not None for i in range(self.n_data)) if data_present: self.blocks_decoded += 1 return list(received_packets[:self.n_data]), True chosen = received_indices[:self.n_data] sub_matrix = [self.encoding_matrix[i][:] for i in chosen] try: inv_matrix = gf_matrix_invert(sub_matrix) except ValueError: self.blocks_failed += 1 return None, False pkt_len = max(len(p) for p in received_packets if p is not None) recovered_data = [bytearray(pkt_len) for _ in range(self.n_data)] chosen_packets = [received_packets[i] for i in chosen] for pos in range(pkt_len): col = [p[pos] if pos < len(p) else 0 for p in chosen_packets] for d in range(self.n_data): val = 0 for k in range(self.n_data): val ^= gf_mul(inv_matrix[d][k], col[k]) recovered_data[d][pos] = val missing_data = sum(1 for i in range(self.n_data) if received_packets[i] is None) self.packets_recovered += missing_data self.blocks_decoded += 1 return [bytes(pkt) for pkt in recovered_data], True
def get_statistics(self): total = self.blocks_decoded + self.blocks_failed rate = (self.blocks_decoded / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0 return { 'blocks_decoded': self.blocks_decoded, 'blocks_failed': self.blocks_failed, 'total_blocks': total, 'success_rate': rate, 'packets_recovered': self.packets_recovered }

module.name: network/channel_model.py
module.purpose: Simulates variable propagation delay of a 5G URLLC link.
module.theory: 5G latency: URLLC ~1ms, eMBB ~4-10ms, congested ~10-50ms. Delay model: delay = base_delay + jitter (normal or uniform distribution). Does NOT actually sleep — annotates packets with simulated delay for offline analysis.

implementation.class: __init__(base_delay_ms=2.0, jitter_std_ms=1.0, distribution='normal', seed=None) get_delay() — samples one delay value, records it get_statistics() — returns count, mean, std, min, max, P50, P95, P99 reset() — clears recorded delays

module.name: network/packet_loss_simulator.py
module.purpose: Simulates packet loss patterns to test FEC effectiveness.
module.theory: Two models: 1. Random (Bernoulli): each packet dropped independently with probability p (i.i.d.) 2. Burst (Gilbert-Elliott): two-state Markov chain (GOOD/BAD states). GOOD state: packets delivered (loss ~0). BAD state: packets dropped (loss ~1). Transitions: GOOD→BAD with prob p_gb, BAD→GOOD with prob p_bg. Average burst length ≈ 1/p_bg. Models real wireless fading.

implementation.class: __init__(loss_rate=0.1, seed=None) apply_loss(packets) — returns list with None replacing dropped packets get_actual_loss_rate() — measured loss rate
__init__(p_good_to_bad=0.05, p_bad_to_good=0.3, p_loss_good=0.0, p_loss_bad=1.0, seed=None) apply_loss(packets) — Markov-chain-based loss simulation get_actual_loss_rate()

module.name: network/traffic_generator.py
module.purpose: Generates real UDP traffic over localhost sockets.

implementation.class: __init__(dest_ip="127.0.0.1", dest_port=5000) send_packets(num_packets, packet_size=1024, delay=0.1) — sends labeled UDP datagrams close()
__init__(listen_ip="127.0.0.1", listen_port=5000) receive_packets(num_packets, timeout=10) — returns list of received data close()

module.name: integration/fec_sender.py
module.purpose: Combines FEC encoder with real UDP sockets for end-to-end FEC transport.
module.theory: Custom packet format: [Block ID: 4B] [Packet Index: 2B] [Total: 2B] [FEC Payload] Buffers incoming data until n_data packets collected, then encodes and sends block.

implementation.class: __init__(dest_ip, dest_port, n_data=4, n_parity=4) send_data(data_payload) — buffers data, encodes + sends when block is full flush() — sends any remaining buffered packets (padded) close()

module.name: integration/fec_receiver.py
module.purpose: Receives FEC-protected UDP data, reassembles blocks, decodes.

implementation.class: __init__(listen_ip, listen_port, n_data=4, n_parity=4) receive_blocks(num_blocks, timeout=30) — receives packets, groups by block_id, attempts decoding when enough packets arrive for a block get_statistics() close()

module.name: experiments/experiment_runner.py
module.purpose: Runs controlled A/B experiments comparing FEC vs no-FEC under varying channel loss conditions.
module.theory: For each loss rate (0%, 5%, 10%, ..., 50%): - Baseline: send N packets, simulate loss, count survivors - With FEC: encode N→(N+K), simulate loss, decode, count recovered Multiple trials per rate for statistical significance. Metrics: PLR, recovery rate, effective throughput, FEC overhead.

implementation.class: __init__(n_data=4, n_parity=4, num_blocks=50, num_trials=5) run_all_experiments(loss_rates) — sweeps 9 loss rates for both random and burst loss _run_loss_sweep(loss_rates, loss_type) — runs all trials at each loss rate _run_single_trial(loss_rate, seed, loss_type) — one complete encode→loss→decode trial _run_latency_experiment() — compares delay with/without FEC save_results(results, filepath) — saves to JSON

module.name: analysis/metrics_collector.py
module.purpose: Aggregates experiment results into formatted ASCII tables.

implementation.class: add_result(channel_loss, no_fec_plr, fec_plr, recovery_rate, overhead, ...) print_summary_table() — formatted results table get_records() — returns raw records

generate_all(results) — calls all 4 plot functions plot_plr_comparison(results) — PLR with/without FEC (random + burst side-by-side) plot_recovery_rate(results) — block recovery rate vs 5G URLLC targets (99.999%) plot_throughput_comparison(results) — effective throughput trade-off plot_latency_cdf(results) — CDF of packet delivery latency
N_DATA_PACKETS = 4 N_PARITY_PACKETS = 4 MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE = 1024 HEADER_SIZE = 16 USE_SYSTEMATIC_CODE = True

demos.demo: Baseline: sends 10 UDP packets with NO FEC. Shows packet loss is permanent.
Sends 1 FEC block (4 data → 8 total). Shows FEC header structure in Wireshark. Header: [Block ID: 4B] [Packet Idx: 2B] [Total: 2B].
Live loss + recovery. On Linux with sudo, uses tc netem for kernel-level 30% loss. Wireshark shows genuinely missing packets. Falls back to application-level simulation.

Best demo for presentations. Sender encodes 8 packets but SKIPS sending packets at LOST_PACKET_INDICES (default [1,3]), simulating network loss. Wireshark shows only 6 packets with gaps in sequence numbers. Receiver runs FEC decoding, recovers the missing data, and verifies byte-for-byte match. Configurable — can lose up to 4 packets (even all 4 data packets) and still recover.

theory_summary.result: Under random loss, FEC reduces effective PLR to near-zero for channel loss up to 40%.
Under burst loss (Gilbert-Elliott), FEC maintains >95% block recovery for loss up to 30%.
FEC introduces 100% bandwidth overhead (code rate 0.5), justified for URLLC where retransmission latency is unacceptable.

context.academic_purpose: This is a final-year / academic project demonstrating how Forward Error Correction can improve reliability in 5G URLLC networks. The project includes a complete implementation from mathematical foundations (GF(256) arithmetic) to network simulation, experimentation, visualization, and live Wireshark demonstrations.
context.file_map: core/galois.py — 132 lines — GF(256) arithmetic + matrix operations core/fec_encoder.py — 125 lines — Cauchy matrix encoding core/fec_decoder.py — 146 lines — Matrix inversion decoding network/channel_model.py — 97 lines — 5G latency simulation network/packet_loss_simulator.py — 150 lines — Random + burst loss models network/traffic_generator.py — 41 lines — UDP packet sender network/traffic_receiver.py — 50 lines — UDP packet receiver integration/fec_sender.py — 76 lines — FEC + UDP sender integration/fec_receiver.py— 96 lines — FEC + UDP receiver experiments/experiment_runner.py — 269 lines — A/B experiment suite analysis/metrics_collector.py — 58 lines — Results aggregation analysis/visualizer.py — 233 lines — Publication-quality graphs (4 types) config/fec_config.py — 26 lines — FEC parameters config/network_config.py — 17 lines — Network parameters demo/demo1_no_fec.py — 101 lines — Baseline demo demo/demo2_with_fec.py — 151 lines — FEC structure demo demo/demo3_live_recovery.py— 404 lines — Live kernel-level loss demo demo/demo4_controlled_recovery.py — 340 lines — Controlled loss + recovery (best demo)
